title: Trace a Resilient Form Design System
summary: Trace a Resilient Form Design System retrieves prior skills in an original community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css form inheritance, css form layout, css form focus, css form errors, css form native custom, css form autofill motion in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab.
steps.why: Trace a Resilient Form Design System makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab.

content.text: Form controls do not inherit every typography and color property consistently across browsers. A bounded normalization can apply font, color, line height, and box sizing while preserving recognizable native affordances, platform states, and high-contrast behavior. Global appearance removal creates a full custom-control obligation. Form layout keeps labels, controls, hints, errors, and actions in a predictable reading and focus sequence. Controls need generous target size, readable measure, wrapping labels, intrinsic widths, and block flow that survives zoom and translation. A compact desktop arrangement cannot simply be scaled down for touch. :focus-visible can expose strong keyboard focus without assuming focus came from one device. :focus-within can reinforce the active group. Indicators need sufficient contrast, thickness, offset, and space so overflow, borders, shadows, and adjacent content do not clip or obscure them. Forced colors may replace author colors, so system-color fallbacks matter. Form states need multiple coordinated cues: specific text, programmatic association, visible boundaries or icons, and color as reinforcement. Help is not an error, required is not invalid, disabled needs explanation, and success must not erase user context. Strong invalid styling should follow interaction or submission rather than blame an untouched form. Native controls include semantics, keyboard interaction, form participation, validation, states, platform conventions, and forced-color behavior. Accent-color and bounded styling often provide enough design control. A custom pattern must retain or rebuild every behavior and keep the real input available to assistive technology without shrinking it to an unusable target. Browsers expose autofill, placeholder, search decorations, and other platform-specific states differently. Styling must keep stored data readable and recognizable rather than fighting the browser. Placeholder remains supporting example text, not a label. Motion around focus, error, and success should be optional and disabled or reduced under prefers-reduced-motion. Apply it to community survey control-state and reflow evidence lab.
